summary: Removed the redundant fighter-side temporary allocation struct `S_TEMP1` and replaced its use with the existing player allocation struct `plAllocInfo`. The change moved `plAllocInfo` from a local definition in `player.c` into `pl/types.h`, updated fighter creation/init prototypes to accept `struct plAllocInfo*`, removed casts from `Player_80031AD0`, and aligned field names/types across player and fighter setup paths.
what_changed: Promoted `struct plAllocInfo` from a private `player.c` definition into `src/melee/pl/types.h` so fighter code can use the real allocation-info type.
Changed `Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914` and `Fighter_Create` signatures from `struct S_TEMP1*` to `struct plAllocInfo*` in implementation, header, and dox declarations.

Updated fighter initialization field accesses from the old temporary names to `plAllocInfo` names: `fighterKind` to `internal_id`, `playerID` to `slot`, `flags_b0` to `b0`, `unk5` to `x5`, and `flags_b1` to `has_transformation`.
Removed explicit casts from `Player_80031AD0` calls to `Fighter_Create`; `first_struct` and `second_struct` are now passed directly as `struct plAllocInfo*`.
Renamed the `plAllocInfo` signed byte field from `unk8` to `x5` in the promoted definition and call sites shown in `player.c`.
Changed `plAllocInfo.internal_id` and `plAllocInfo2.internal_id` to use `FighterKind` rather than plain `s32` in `pl/types.h`.
Updated `ftdemo.c` temporary setup to instantiate `struct plAllocInfo` instead of `struct S_TEMP1` before calling `Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914`.
smart_moves: Eliminated an unsafe compatibility cast by making the callee accept the actual producer-side allocation struct.
Kept `fighter.h` independent by forward-declaring `struct plAllocInfo` instead of pulling in the full player header unnecessarily.
Moved the shared struct definition to `pl/types.h`, which is a more appropriate location for player allocation data than a private `player.c` local definition or a duplicate fighter temp struct.
Used `FighterKind` for `internal_id`, improving type specificity for fighter identity fields.
Updated `fighter.dox` alongside headers and implementation, keeping documentation declarations synchronized with code.
decomp_lessons: When two structs have the same layout and are connected only by casts, prefer consolidating to one named struct rather than preserving a fake temporary type.
A cast marked with a TODO such as `Eliminate cast` can be a strong clue that the callee prototype is using the wrong decomp-era placeholder type.
Promoting a local struct into a shared header can resolve cross-module type identity without changing runtime behavior.
Forward declarations are useful for function prototypes that only need pointers to shared structs, reducing include coupling.
Field renames during type consolidation should preserve the observed layout and bit positions; here the bitfield meaning `has_transformation` replaced a generic `flags_b1` access.
Prefer semantic enum-like typedefs such as `FighterKind` over raw integer types when the field clearly stores a fighter kind/internal id.

naming_conventions.lesson: Avoid anonymous-looking temporary names once a better subsystem-owned struct name exists.
Use the existing player allocation field name when consolidating with `plAllocInfo`.
The allocation input field represents the player slot in the promoted struct.
Unknown byte fields may retain offset-style names when semantics are not yet known.
Bitfield names from the canonical struct should be reused rather than maintaining duplicate flag aliases.
Use semantic bitfield names when the meaning is known.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was primarily a type/header cleanup rather than an assembly matching rewrite; no explicit asm tricks or matching constraints were provided in the PR context.

The change likely preserves codegen by replacing layout-compatible structs and field accesses without altering control flow or data layout; confidence is based on the diff, not on an explicit reviewer statement.
Removing casts after unifying prototypes is a safe decomp tactic when the underlying field offsets and bitfields remain equivalent.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided excerpt.
The PR merged quickly with no visible review-comment evidence in the supplied context.
follow_up_queries: Find other casts between player allocation structs and fighter allocation inputs that may indicate duplicate placeholder structs.
Compare `plAllocInfo` and `plAllocInfo2` layouts to determine whether the TODO saying they are probably the same struct can be resolved.
Investigate the semantic meaning of `plAllocInfo.x5` now that it is shared with fighter initialization via `fp->x61C`.
Search for remaining `S_TEMP1` references to confirm full removal.
Audit uses of `b0` in `plAllocInfo` to determine whether a more semantic name is known.
